claims.text: Previously, it has been shown that ECM degradation causes marked instability in dendritic spines and promotes synaptic plasticity (De Vivo et al., 2013; Orlando et al., 2012).
claims.raw_text: Previously, it has been shown that ECM degradation causes marked instability in dendritic spines and promotes synaptic plasticity (De Vivo et al., 2013; Orlando et al., 2012).
support_evidence_snippet: Previously, it has been shown that ECM degradation causes marked instability in dendritic spines and promotes synaptic plasticity (De Vivo et al., 2013; Orlando et al., 2012).

claims.text: The intricate connection between synapses and the ECM in which they reside suggests that changes in the ECM may affect their function, and their interaction with microglia.
claims.raw_text: The intricate connection between synapses and the ECM in which they reside suggests that changes in the ECM may affect their function, and their interaction with microglia.
support_evidence_snippet: The intricate connection between synapses and the ECM in which they reside suggests that changes in the ECM may affect their function, and their interaction with microglia.

claims.text: Furthermore, it has recently been shown that adult microglia degrade perisynaptic ECM proteins (aggrecan and brevican) to promote memory consolidation in response to neuronal IL-33 release (Nguyen et al., 2020).
claims.raw_text: Furthermore, it has recently been shown that adult microglia degrade perisynaptic ECM proteins (aggrecan and brevican) to promote memory consolidation in response to neuronal IL-33 release (Nguyen et al., 2020).
support_evidence_snippet: Furthermore, it has recently been shown that adult microglia degrade perisynaptic ECM proteins (aggrecan and brevican) to promote memory consolidation in response to neuronal IL-33 release (Nguyen et al., 2020).

claims.text: Our previous work suggests that ECM controls microglial behavior in the early stages of amyloidosis (Stoyanov et al., 2021).
claims.raw_text: Our previous work suggests that ECM controls microglial behavior in the early stages of amyloidosis (Stoyanov et al., 2021).
support_evidence_snippet: Our previous work suggests that ECM controls microglial behavior in the early stages of amyloidosis (Stoyanov et al., 2021).

claims.text: In vivo imaging in the RSC and targeted laser photoablation (Cangalaya et al., 2020) demonstrated that ECM degradation mitigates spine loss, microglial contact with spines, and complement protein deposition.
claims.raw_text: In vivo imaging in the RSC and targeted laser photoablation (Cangalaya et al., 2020) demonstrated that ECM degradation mitigates spine loss, microglial contact with spines, and complement protein deposition.
support_evidence_snippet: In vivo imaging in the RSC and targeted laser photoablation (Cangalaya et al., 2020) demonstrated that ECM degradation mitigates spine loss, microglial contact with spines, and complement protein deposition.

claims.text: Previous studies have demonstrated the effectiveness of Ch ABC injection in ECM digestion by removing chondroitin sulfate side chains at 1, 3, and 7 days postinjection (Hayani et al., 2018; Stoyanov et al., 2021).
claims.raw_text: Previous studies have demonstrated the effectiveness of Ch ABC injection in ECM digestion by removing chondroitin sulfate side chains at 1, 3, and 7 days postinjection (Hayani et al., 2018; Stoyanov et al., 2021).
support_evidence_snippet: Previous studies have demonstrated the effectiveness of Ch ABC injection in ECM digestion by removing chondroitin sulfate side chains at 1, 3, and 7 days postinjection (Hayani et al., 2018; Stoyanov et al., 2021).

claims.text: These data show that the enzymatic digestion of ECM alters the synaptic remodeling associated with PD and prevents the elimination of spines.
claims.raw_text: These data show that the enzymatic digestion of ECM alters the synaptic remodeling associated with PD and prevents the elimination of spines.
support_evidence_snippet: These data show that the enzymatic digestion of ECM alters the synaptic remodeling associated with PD and prevents the elimination of spines.

claims.text: Collectively, these results suggest that the neural ECM plays a crucial role in synaptic remodeling pr ocesses carried out by microglia in response to local synaptic damage in a healthy adult brain.
claims.raw_text: Collectively, these results suggest that the neural ECM plays a crucial role in synaptic remodeling pr ocesses carried out by microglia in response to local synaptic damage in a healthy adult brain.
support_evidence_snippet: Collectively, these results suggest that the neural ECM plays a crucial role in synaptic remodeling pr ocesses carried out by microglia in response to local synaptic damage in a healthy adult brain.

claims.text: Our findings suggest that ECM integrity is crucial for microglia-dependent structural plasticity and for tissue deposition of eat-me signals.
claims.raw_text: Our findings suggest that ECM integrity is crucial for microglia-dependent structural plasticity and for tissue deposition of eat-me signals.
support_evidence_snippet: Our findings suggest that ECM integrity is crucial for microglia-dependent structural plasticity and for tissue deposition of eat-me signals.

claims.text: This activity is presumably aimed at engulfing remaining ECM products as our data show that microglia engulf CSPGs.
claims.raw_text: This activity is presumably aimed at engulfing remaining ECM products as our data show that microglia engulf CSPGs.
support_evidence_snippet: This activity is presumably aimed at engulfing remaining ECM products as our data show that microglia engulf CSPGs.

claims.text: Using synaptic photodamage, we show that synaptic turnover was reduced when the integrity of the ECM was compromised.
claims.raw_text: Using synaptic photodamage, we show that synaptic turnover was reduced when the integrity of the ECM was compromised.
support_evidence_snippet: Using synaptic photodamage, we show that synaptic turnover was reduced when the integrity of the ECM was compromised.

claims.text: These results suggest that neural/ perisynaptic ECM loss may reduce the signals that contribute to synaptic remodeling by microglia.
claims.raw_text: These results suggest that neural/ perisynaptic ECM loss may reduce the signals that contribute to synaptic remodeling by microglia.
support_evidence_snippet: These results suggest that neural/ perisynaptic ECM loss may reduce the signals that contribute to synaptic remodeling by microglia.

evidence.quote: ChABC treatment did not change the microglial somatic area and circularity compared with the control group at D1 and D7 (Figure 2d,e). However, at D7, microglia from the ChABC group demonstrated more prominently ramified morphology with abundant branched and filamentous cell processes in comparison with the control group (Figure 2e). Branch length was significantly increased in the ChABC group at D7 when compared to the vehicle group (314.30 ± 40.57 μm vs. 488.53 ± 29.02 μm, p = .022) (Figure 2h). The elongated processes and increased branching complexity of microglia could be indicative of a “regulatory” phenotype in response to nonthreatening stimuli such as disaccharides and proteins generated from the degradation of CSPGs (Ebert et al., 2008). figures.caption: ChABC treatment did not change the microglial somatic area and circularity compared with the control group at D1 and D7 (Figure 2d,e). However, at D7, microglia from the ChABC group demonstrated more prominently ramified morphology with abundant branched and filamentous cell processes in comparison with the control group (Figure 2e). Branch length was significantly increased in the ChABC group at D7 when compared to the vehicle group (314.30 ± 40.57 μm vs. 488.53 ± 29.02 μm, p = .022) (Figure 2h). The elongated processes and increased branching complexity of microglia could be indicative of a “regulatory” phenotype in response to nonthreatening stimuli such as disaccharides and proteins generated from the degradation of CSPGs (Ebert et al., 2008).
